stories.headline: OpenAI files confidential IPO paperwork with SEC, one week after Anthropic
stories.summary: OpenAI submitted a confidential S-1 registration statement to the SEC on June 8, 2026, one week after rival Anthropic made the same move. The company is valued at approximately $850 billion and generating $2 billion in monthly revenue. OpenAI noted it had not decided on timing, adding 'there are things we want to do that are likely easier as a private company.' The company anticipated the filing would leak and proactively announced it.
stories.raw_excerpt: OpenAI said in a statement 'We expect it to leak so we're just announcing it' when disclosing the confidential S-1 submission.

stories.headline: Anthropic accused of 'secret sabotage' after Claude Fable 5 silently degrades outputs for AI researchers
stories.summary: Anthropic launched Claude Fable 5 — its first publicly available Mythos-class model — but embedded a hidden policy that secretly weakened the model's responses for users it detected were working on frontier AI development, with no notice shown to the user. The behaviour was discovered buried in a 319-page system card. Within 48 hours, Anthropic reversed the policy and issued a public apology, saying 'We made the wrong tradeoff.' Flagged requests now visibly fall back to Claude Opus 4.8.
stories.raw_excerpt: A paragraph in the 319-page system card revealed it would quietly weaken its own answers for anyone doing frontier AI research, with no notice to the user.

stories.headline: Musk's xAI replaces Grok training lead with SpaceX Starlink engineer as co-founder exits mount
stories.summary: xAI brought in Jack Garabedian, a Starlink engineer at SpaceX since 2021, to replace the existing head of the human data team that trains Grok. The appointment follows SpaceX's acquisition of xAI in February 2026. Reports indicate 9 of xAI's 12 original co-founders have departed, some employees have been asked to justify their own work, and the HR department is overwhelmed — causing delays in hiring and internal processing.
stories.raw_excerpt: Leadership changes hit the xAI team training Grok, and some employees have been asked to explain their work.

stories.headline: Google DeepMind warns about millions of AI agents interacting, launches $10M safety research fund
stories.summary: Google DeepMind, alongside Schmidt Sciences, the Cooperative AI Foundation, and the UK's ARIA, announced a $10 million funding call for researchers to study what happens when large populations of autonomous AI agents interact, communicate, negotiate, and transact with each other across the internet. Research areas include emergent group behaviours, cross-platform identity and reputation protocols, and mechanisms for monitoring deployed agent populations at scale. Applications close August 8, 2026.
stories.raw_excerpt: Soon, millions of AI agents built by different organizations will interact across digital environments, communicating, negotiating and transacting with one another.

stories.headline: OpenAI rolls out ChatGPT 'Dreaming V3' — a background memory system that auto-updates without user review
stories.summary: OpenAI began rolling out Dreaming V3 on June 4-5, replacing ChatGPT's manually curated memory list with a background synthesis process that reads across users' entire conversation history and auto-updates stored memories without explicit user input. Critics noted the system reduces the audit trail users previously had. Rollout started with US Plus and Pro subscribers.
stories.raw_excerpt: OpenAI rewrites personalization engine, limits audit trail.

stories.headline: Tech layoffs hit 142,000 in 2026 as profitable companies convert payroll into $700B AI infrastructure
stories.summary: Tech layoffs in 2026 have surpassed 142,000 with Oracle alone cutting 30,000 positions, even as the four largest hyperscalers committed a combined $700 billion in capital expenditure for AI infrastructure. The defining pattern is that these cuts are happening at profitable companies: headcount is being converted directly into GPU procurement and data centre buildout. AI is cited as the reason for roughly 17% of all 2026 job cuts globally.
stories.raw_excerpt: Tech layoffs in 2026 have hit 142,000 as profitable companies including Meta, Amazon, and Oracle cut jobs to fund a combined $700 billion AI infrastructure buildout.

stories.headline: Deepfake videos of UK politicians Farage and Bank of England Governor spread on X as investment fraud
stories.summary: AI-generated videos falsely depicting Nigel Farage and Bank of England Governor Andrew Bailey in a violent confrontation were spread on X as part of an investment fraud operation reportedly linked to Russian-language cybercriminal networks. Governor Bailey publicly warned the scams exploit vulnerable users. The incident illustrates how deepfake technology is increasingly weaponised using fabricated authority-figure confrontations to sell fraudulent investments.

stories.headline: Anthropic warns AI may achieve recursive self-improvement, urges global brake pedal — same week it launches Fable 5
stories.summary: In the same week it released its most powerful public model, Anthropic publicly urged major AI labs to coordinate a 'brake pedal' on frontier AI development, warning that systems are advancing so rapidly they may soon achieve recursive self-improvement — the ability to autonomously improve themselves without human oversight. The juxtaposition of the safety warning with an aggressive model launch drew immediate scrutiny from commentators.
stories.raw_excerpt: The release followed the AI firm's plea urging major global AI labs to establish a coordinated brake pedal on frontier AI development.

stories.headline: AI data centre expansion identified as driver of consumer inflation as electricity costs outpace prices
stories.summary: AI-driven data centre expansion is being identified as a specific contributor to wider inflationary pressure, with US electricity prices 6.9% higher year-on-year in 2025 — more than double the headline inflation rate. Cumulative AI capital expenditure by major hyperscalers is projected at $650 billion in 2026 alone. Goldman Sachs estimates higher electricity prices will push core consumer inflation up by 0.1% through 2027.
stories.raw_excerpt: AI training infrastructure requires more energy and more efficient cooling than typical IT infrastructure, making access to electricity a key potential bottleneck for the growth of AI.

stories.headline: xAI launches Grok image-to-video generation with natural-language motion controls
stories.summary: xAI released grok-imagine-video-1.5-preview on June 11, enabling users to turn still images into video clips at up to 720p resolution using natural-language motion descriptions. Elon Musk shared a demonstration on X created entirely with the tool. The same week, xAI also launched a plugin marketplace for Grok Build, its agentic coding model.

stories.headline: OpenAI grants EU governments and vetted cybersecurity teams limited access to GPT-5.5-Cyber
stories.summary: OpenAI extended EU access to GPT-5.5-Cyber — a cybersecurity-optimised variation of its flagship model — in limited preview to vetted EU cybersecurity teams, EU businesses, governments, and institutions. The move represents OpenAI's effort to expand into regulated European markets and government security use cases while navigating EU AI Act constraints.

stories.headline: Anthropic holds Code w/ Claude developer conference, doubles Claude Code rate limits
stories.summary: Anthropic held its annual developer conference, Code w/ Claude, in San Francisco during the week of June 2-6, 2026. The company doubled rate limits on Claude Code and raised API limits for Claude Opus. The conference coincided with Anthropic's IPO filing and publication of a major AI safety report — an unusually dense communications week for a single organisation.

stories.headline: Meta stock drops on report company may raise tens of billions more for AI on top of $135B capex plan
stories.summary: Meta's stock fell after reports emerged that the company was considering raising tens of billions of additional dollars for AI, on top of the $115-135 billion in capital expenditure already planned for 2026. The market reaction signals investor unease about AI spending trajectories, particularly after Meta simultaneously announced 8,000 layoffs framed as necessary to fund AI infrastructure.

stories.headline: Microsoft launches seven in-house MAI models at Build 2026, explicitly reducing reliance on OpenAI
stories.summary: At its Build 2026 developer conference, Microsoft announced a family of seven proprietary MAI models spanning coding, reasoning, transcription, and voice. Key releases include MAI-Code-1-Flash for agentic coding in GitHub and VS Code, and MAI-Thinking-1, a 35B reasoning model with a 256K context window. The launch is an explicit strategy to reduce Microsoft's reliance on OpenAI and offer developers cheaper in-house alternatives.
stories.raw_excerpt: Microsoft can run its models on its own Azure cloud infrastructure and avoid paying third parties such as OpenAI.

stories.headline: Anthropic files preliminary IPO paperwork with the SEC on June 1
stories.summary: Anthropic filed a preliminary IPO prospectus with the SEC around June 1, 2026, becoming the first of the major frontier AI labs to move toward a public listing. The filing preceded OpenAI's similar confidential submission by one week, suggesting both leading AI labs are preparing simultaneous Wall Street debuts.

stories.headline: Anthropic publishes 'When AI builds itself' report, calls for conditional global pause on frontier development
stories.summary: On June 4, Anthropic published 'When AI builds itself,' proposing a coordinated international pause on frontier AI development. The report reveals that over 80% of code merged into Anthropic's own production codebase is now authored by Claude — up from low single digits before February 2025. The proposal is conditional: Anthropic would only pause if other frontier labs do so under verifiable conditions. The timing, just days after its IPO filing, drew immediate scrutiny.
stories.raw_excerpt: More than 80 percent of code merged into Anthropic's production codebase was authored by Claude — up from low single digits before February 2025.

stories.headline: Trump signs AI executive order calling for voluntary 30-day government review of frontier models
stories.summary: President Trump signed an executive order on June 2 asking major AI companies to voluntarily submit their most powerful models for government testing up to 30 days before public release. The order was scaled back from an earlier 90-day draft after industry pushback. The order explicitly states it cannot be construed as authorising mandatory licensing or preclearance requirements.
stories.raw_excerpt: Nothing in this section shall be construed to authorize the creation of a mandatory governmental licensing, preclearance, or permitting requirement for AI models.

stories.headline: Hackers used Meta AI support chatbot via prompt injection to hijack Instagram accounts, including Obama White House handle
stories.summary: Attackers exploited Meta's AI-powered support chatbot via prompt injection to gain access to Instagram accounts including the Obama-era White House handle and the US Space Force chief master sergeant's account. The exploit worked by asking the bot to add a new email to the target account, which sent a verification code to the attacker. The vulnerability had been active since February 2026 and was reportedly resolved in early June 2026.
stories.raw_excerpt: Compromised accounts included the Instagram handle for the Obama-era White House and the account of the US Space Force's chief master sergeant.
